Description / Instructions: A bodyweight exercise focusing on wrists, chest, triceps, building strength and control without external load.
How To Perform 1: Hand Placement: Place your hands together directly under your chest, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers touching. This position targets your triceps and inner chest.
How To Perform 2: Body Alignment: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core to prevent your hips from sagging or rising.
How To Perform 3: Elbow Position: As you lower yourself, keep your elbows close to your body. This will maximize triceps engagement and reduce strain on your shoulders.
How To Perform 4: Controlled Movement: Lower your chest towards the diamond slowly, aiming to touch it or get as close as possible. Push back up explosively but with control, ensuring you maintain that straight body line.

How To Perform 2: Lift your legs and torso off the bench in one solid motion, keeping your body straight like a lever.
How To Perform 3: Slowly lower back down without arching your back, maintaining full-body tension.

How To Perform 3: Slowly lower your legs and torso back down, keeping the tension in your core.

How To Perform 3: Slowly lower your body back down, controlling the descent to keep tension in your core.

Description / Instructions: A dynamic hamstring exercise
How To Perform 1: Do keep your hips extended – Squeeze your glutes and keep your hips up throughout the rep. Think "glutes like headlights," not sinking ships.
How To Perform 2: Do control both directions – Don’t just fling your legs in and out. Eccentric (lengthening) phase is where the magic (and the DOMS) happens.
How To Perform 3: Do engage your core – Ribs down, abs on. Keeps your spine from turning into a banana during the movement.

Exercise Display / Reference Name: Single Leg Hamstring Curls on Sliders (Negatives)
Tags / Target Areas: Hips, Hamstrings
Description / Instructions: A dynamic hamstring exercise
How To Perform 1: Set up for Single Leg Hamstring Curls on Sliders (Negatives). Establish proper hand or foot placement and engage your core before starting.
How To Perform 2: Execute the movement with full body tension. Quality of each repetition matters more than speed.
How To Perform 3: Do engage your core – Ribs down, abs on. Keeps your spine from turning into a banana during the movement.
How To Perform 4: Don’t let your hips drop – If your bum’s sagging, your hammies aren't bragging. Keep those hips up. Don’t rush the movement – Fast = sloppy. Sliders expose everything, so earn your reps with control. Don’t go full range if you can’t maintain form – Half a solid rep is better than a full rep that looks like you’re auditioning for a floor is lava game.
